name: Yom Kippur
traditions: 25-hour fast; intensive synagogue prayers; Kol Nidre evening service; wearing white; abstaining from work, leather shoes, and bathing.
historicalBackground: Yom Kippur is the holiest day in Judaism, established in the Torah. It is the Day of Atonement when Jews seek forgiveness from God for sins of the past year.
description: The Day of Atonement, the holiest day in the Jewish calendar dedicated to fasting, prayer, and spiritual reflection.
